target.title: test(playback): guard repeated barge-in audio_end_ms against regression
target.body:
## Summary

`BUGS-ANALYSIS.md` noted that a second `speech_started` event could send `audio_end_ms` larger than the item's actual audio length, causing an OpenAI API error. The referenced implementation used `lastAssistantItemId` / `lastAssistantItemStartedAtMs` variables that are no longer present — the playback tracker refactoring already addressed the root cause:

- `clear()` resets both `pending` and `playedMsByItem` on every barge-in, so accumulated played-ms from a previous item can never carry into the next one.
- `truncation()` caps `playedWithinChunkMs` at `chunk.durationMs`, so elapsed wall-clock time beyond the chunk's audio length is safely clamped.
- A stale Twilio mark arriving after `clear()` hits an empty `pending` list, returns index -1, and is ignored — it cannot corrupt `playedMsByItem`.

No code change is required. This PR adds two regression tests that encode the invariant and would catch any future refactoring that re-introduces the bug.

## Real behavior proof

Tests run against the playback tracker directly — no OpenAI session needed:

```
# Subtest: audio_end_ms never exceeds item audio length after repeated barge-ins
ok 1 - audio_end_ms never exceeds item audio length after repeated barge-ins
# Subtest: playedMsByItem is fully reset on clear so repeated barge-ins start fresh
ok 2 - playedMsByItem is fully reset on clear so repeated barge-ins start fresh
```

The worst case is modelled explicitly: item X with three 300ms chunks (two marks acked = 600ms accumulated), then `clear()`, then item Y with two 200ms chunks (one mark acked = 200ms accumulated), and a media clock far beyond Y's total duration. `audio_end_ms` is asserted ≤ 400ms in both runs.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm run lint`
- [x] `pnpm run typecheck`
- [x] `pnpm test` — 58/58 passing, including the two new regression tests
- [x] `pnpm run build`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: joevenner
